idx_title: Metronome — A premium metronome for working musicians
idx_meta_desc: Sample-accurate timing, polyrhythms, mixed meter, MIDI clock, and Ableton Link — wrapped in 11 hand-crafted themes. Now on the App Store.
idx_og_desc: Sample-accurate timing, polyrhythms, mixed meter, MIDI clock, and Ableton Link.
idx_hero_tagline: Sample-accurate timing, pro rhythm tools, and a finish worthy of stage and studio.
idx_screenshot_alt: Metronome app screen showing tempo controls, time signature, and beat indicators
idx_features_sub: Designed for working musicians who care about timing — at home, in the studio, and on stage.
idx_feat1_p: Dedicated audio scheduling. No drift. No stutter. No surprises mid-take.
idx_feat2_p: Chain 5/8 → 7/8 → 4/4 freely. Layer 3:2, 4:3, 5:4 with multi-bar resolution.
idx_feat3_p: Subtle micro-timing variation that breathes like a real drummer — not a grid.
idx_feat4_p: Sync with hardware, apps, and other devices. Production-ready in studio or on stage.
idx_feat5_p: Control playback from your wrist. Glance at tempo from the Lock Screen.
idx_feat6_p: Five dark, five light, plus System. Stage Mode for dim venues. Custom click sounds.
sup_meta_desc: Help, contact, and FAQ for the Metronome iOS app.
sup_tagline: Help, contact, and answers for working musicians.
sup_contact_intro: For bug reports, feature requests, or anything else, email us:
sup_contact_reply: We aim to reply within 2 business days.
sup_faq1_q: The click feels late or early — even after calibration.
sup_faq1_a: Bluetooth headphones add 150–300 ms via codec, and the value drifts with battery and proximity. After running All Settings → Latency Calibration, also disable spatial audio and any EQ presets on your headphones — those layer additional latency on top.
sup_faq2_q: How is Human Feel different from a swing percentage?
sup_faq2_a: Swing skews subdivisions in a fixed pattern (long-short, long-short). Human Feel adds randomized micro-timing variation across all subdivisions, weighted so downbeats stay rock-solid while off-beats breathe. The strength slider controls how much — at 100%, it's roughly what a session drummer adds without thinking about it.
sup_faq3_a: Link only transmits beat phase and tempo, not count-ins. If Metronome is the Link source, your DAW joins on the next beat boundary; if the DAW is the source, Metronome's count-in plays locally before beat 1.
sup_faq4_q: Can I import custom click sounds?
sup_faq4_a: Yes — drop a 16-bit stereo WAV at 44.1 kHz with minimal leading silence (≤1 ms) into Metronome's folder via the Files app, then assign it in Sound Library. Other formats will play, but extra leading silence will shift the perceived downbeat.
sup_faq5_q: How do I unlock Mixed Meter, Polyrhythm, or Human Feel?
sup_faq5_a: One-time Pro purchase. No subscription. Tap any locked feature for the upgrade sheet, or Restore Purchases if you've bought it before.
sup_faq6_q: How do I delete all my data?
sup_faq6_a: All Settings → Delete All Data. Wipes practice log, streaks, goals, setlists, and imported sounds. Immediate and irreversible.
sup_faq7_q: Can I bind volume buttons to BPM?
sup_faq7_a: Yes — All Settings → BPM Controls → Volume Buttons = BPM. Off by default so it doesn't override normal volume behavior unexpectedly. While enabled, ringer volume is unaffected.
sup_faq8_q: MIDI Clock or Ableton Link won't connect.
sup_faq8_a: Both need local-network and Bluetooth permissions in iOS Settings → Metronome. Link also requires all devices to be on the same Wi-Fi network — and the same VLAN, if your router uses guest-network isolation.
sup_privacy_p1: Metronome does not collect, sell, or share your personal data. The app stores your settings, practice log, and setlists on your device only. Optional iCloud sync uses your private Apple iCloud account — the developer and any third party have no access.
sup_privacy_p2: Microphone access is used only by the built-in tuner to detect pitch in real time; audio is processed on-device and never recorded or transmitted.
priv_meta_desc: Privacy policy for the Metronome iOS app — what we don't collect, what stays on your device, and how to reach us.
priv_summary_p: Metronome does not collect, store, or share your personal data on any server. There is no account system, no analytics, no advertising, and no third-party tracking. Everything you do in the app stays on your device.
priv_s1_intro: Metronome does not collect any of the following:
priv_s1_li1: Personal identifiers (name, email, phone number, account ID)
priv_s1_li2: Device identifiers used for tracking (IDFA, IDFV)
priv_s1_li5: Crash reports beyond the anonymous reports Apple provides via TestFlight or App Analytics, which you can opt out of in iOS Settings
priv_s1_li6: Audio recordings, MIDI data, or any media content
priv_s2_intro: The app stores the following locally on your iPhone, iPad, or Apple Watch. None of this leaves the device unless you explicitly enable iCloud sync (see Section 4):
priv_s2_li1: App settings (selected theme, sounds, latency calibration, BPM, time signature)
priv_s2_delete: You can delete all of this data at any time from All Settings → Delete All Data. The deletion is immediate and cannot be undone.
priv_s3_mic_p: Used by the built-in tuner to detect the pitch of your instrument in real time. Audio is analyzed on-device and discarded immediately — nothing is recorded, stored, or transmitted. The microphone is only active while the tuner screen is open.
priv_s3_bt_p: Used to connect to Bluetooth MIDI devices for clock output and external control. Metronome does not scan for or connect to non-MIDI Bluetooth devices.
priv_s3_ln_p: Used by Ableton Link to discover and sync tempo with other music apps and devices on the same Wi-Fi network. Metronome does not transmit any data to the public internet via this permission.
priv_s4_p: If you enable iCloud sync, your setlists, practice log, and settings are synchronized across your own Apple devices using Apple's iCloud infrastructure. The data is stored in your private Apple ID container, encrypted in transit and at rest by Apple. The developer of Metronome cannot access this data, and no third party is involved. If you disable iCloud sync, the data remains on the device where you created it.
priv_s5_p: Pro features and tip-jar purchases are handled by Apple's StoreKit. Apple processes the transaction; the developer receives only the standard receipt (anonymous purchase confirmation). Metronome does not see your payment details, name, or Apple ID.
priv_s6_p: Metronome is suitable for users of all ages and does not knowingly collect any personal data from children under 13. Because the app does not collect personal data from any user, no special children's data flow exists. If you believe a child has somehow provided personal data to us, please contact us and we will confirm there is nothing to delete on our side.
priv_rights_h2: 7. Your rights under GDPR and CCPA
priv_rights_intro: If you are in the European Economic Area, the United Kingdom, Switzerland, or California, you have rights to access, correct, delete, and port your personal data, and to object to or restrict processing. Because Metronome does not collect or process personal data on any server, the practical answer to all of these is the same: there is no server-side record of you.
priv_rights_li1: Right of access and portability. All of your data is stored on your device, where you can view it in the app at any time.
priv_rights_li2: Right to erasure. Use All Settings → Delete All Data in the app. The deletion is immediate.
priv_rights_li3: Right to opt out of sale or sharing. We do not sell or share personal data, period.
priv_thirdparty_p1: The only third party involved in Metronome is Apple: App Store distribution, StoreKit purchases, and optional iCloud sync are all provided by Apple and governed by Apple's privacy policy.
priv_thirdparty_p2: No other third-party SDK, server, analytics provider, or advertising network is integrated into the app.
priv_s7_p: If this policy changes in a meaningful way, the "Last updated" date at the top will be revised and a summary of the change will be added to a new section here. Continued use of the app after a policy change constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
priv_s8_p_after: . We aim to reply within 2 business days.
trm_meta_desc: Terms of use for the Metronome iOS app — license, the one-time Pro purchase, and acceptable use, in plain English.
trm_summary_p: Metronome is free to download and use. One optional one-time purchase — Metronomad Pro — unlocks the advanced rhythm tools: no subscription, no account, no recurring charges. Apple handles all payments, and your data stays on your device. These terms are the plain-English rules for using the app.
trm_s1_p: By downloading or using Metronome, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree with them, please do not use the app.
trm_s2_p: Metronome is licensed to you, not sold, under Apple's standard Licensed Application End User License Agreement (EULA). These terms supplement Apple's EULA; if they conflict, Apple's EULA controls. The license lets you use the app on any Apple device you own or control, as permitted by the App Store Usage Rules.
trm_s3_p1: The app is free, with a single optional in-app purchase: Metronomad Pro ($3.99, or the equivalent in your local currency) — a one-time, non-recurring unlock. There are no subscriptions, no recurring charges, and no ads.
trm_s3_p2: Payment is billed by Apple to your Apple ID. If you reinstall the app or move to a new device, tap Restore Purchases in the app to unlock Pro again at no extra cost. Refunds are handled by Apple, not the developer — you can request one at reportaproblem.apple.com.
trm_s4_h2: 4. Your content stays on your device
trm_s4_p: Custom click sounds you import and the practice data the app records (practice log, streaks, goals, setlists) stay on your device and remain yours. The developer never receives a copy. Please make sure you have the rights to any audio you import. See the Privacy Policy for exactly what is stored and how to delete it.
trm_s5_p: Use Metronome as it is intended: as a metronome and practice tool. Don't copy, resell, or redistribute the app or its bundled sounds outside what Apple's EULA allows, don't attempt to bypass the Pro unlock, and don't use the app in any way that breaks the law.
trm_s6_p: Metronome is provided "as is". We work hard on timing accuracy and stability, but we cannot promise the app will always be error-free, and we make no warranties beyond those required by law.
trm_s7_p: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the developer is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the app, and total liability for any claim is limited to the amount you paid for the app. Some jurisdictions do not allow these limitations, so they may not fully apply to you.
trm_s8_p: If these terms change in a meaningful way, the "Last updated" date at the top will be revised. Continued use of the app after a change constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
trm_s9_p_after: . We aim to reply within 2 business days.
